title: Dryopteris decurrens
taxonomy: Dryopteris decurrens belongs to the kingdom Plantae, further classified under the division Pteridophyta, and specifically to the class Polypodiopsida. Within this class, it falls under the order Polypodiales, and then the family Dryopteridaceae. Further classification includes the subfamily Dryopteridoideae and the genus Dryopteris. The species D. decurrens is a type of fern, which is a common classification for plants within the Dryopteridaceae family. This classification provides a comprehensive understanding of the plant's relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.
uses: Dryopteris decurrens, also known as the creeping wood fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant has been employed to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Dryopteris decurrens, commonly known as the creeping wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris decurrens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the creeping wood fern is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support skin and wound healing.
